# Simon Olsson
# Simon Olsson

> Associate Professor at Chalmers University of Technology, Gothenburg, Sweden.
> Head of the AIMLeNS (AI and Machine Learning in the Natural Sciences) lab.
> Research focus: machine learning for molecular simulation, generative AI for 
> drug and antibody design, and AI-driven inverse molecular design.

## About

Simon Olsson leads the AIMLeNS group at the Data Science and AI division of 
Chalmers' Computer Science and Engineering department. His work sits at the 
interface of AI/ML and the natural sciences, with a particular focus on 
generative AI surrogates for molecular dynamics and the design of small-molecule 
therapeutics, biologics, vaccines, and antibodies.

He is a WASP AI/MLX Fellow, ELLIS Member and is funded by the ERC, Knut and Alice Wallenberg 
Foundation, WASP, Data-Driven Life Sciences (DDLS), WACQT, and AstraZeneca. 
Simon is the inaugural recipient of the ICTP-IBM Brahmagupta AI Prize.

## Key Research Areas

- Generative AI for molecular simulation (flow matching, Boltzmann generators)
- Inverse molecular design (small molecules, biologics, antibodies)
- Implicit transfer operators and surrogate models for MD
- Markov state models and molecular kinetics

## Selected Publications

- Noé, Olsson et al. "Boltzmann generators: Sampling equilibrium states of 
  many-body systems with deep learning." Science, 2019.
- Olsson & Noé. "Dynamic graphical models of molecular kinetics." PNAS, 2019.
- Schreiner, Winther & Olsson. "Implicit Transfer Operator Learning: Multiple Time-Resolution Models for Molecular Dynamics." NeurIPS, 2023.
- Diez, Schreiner & Olsson. "Transferable Generative Models Bridge Femtosecond to Nanosecond Time-Step Molecular Dynamics." Science, 2023.
- Irwin, Tibo, Janet & Olsson. "Efficient 3D Molecular Generation with Flow 
  Matching and Scale Optimal Transport." 2025 AISTATS.
